content:
Time allowed: Two HoursUniversity of Jaffna, Sri Lanka
Bachelor of Science Degree Examination, Level 3G - 2021
(End of Course Examination)
Computer Science
CSC301G3: Rapid Application Development
Answer the First Question and Any Two of the Other Three
This paper has 4 questions in a total of 3 pages
1. Rapid Application Development (RAD) refers to a development life cycle designed to give much
faster development and higher quality systems than the traditional life cycle.
(a) Briefly describe the following pillars of RAD in your own words: [15%]
i. Tools
ii. Methodology
iii. Management
(b) State four benefits of RAD compared with traditional software development life cycle. [08%]
(c) State four stages of a typical RAD life cycle. With the help of diagrams clearly show the
sub-stages and tasks in each stages of RAD life cycle. [16%]
(d) Conversion to a system is an approach to implementing a system to physically convert an
old information system to a new or modified one. Explain the conversion technique direct
changeover and describe why direct changeover is considered a risky approach to conversion.[06%]
(e) Suppose you were asked to lead a Joint Application Design (JAD) session. List five guide-
lines you would follow in playing the proper role as a JAD session leader. [05%]
[50% Marks]
2. Interviewing is an important method for collecting data on information system requirement.
(a) Write three ways of structuring interviews and briefly describe how each of these basic
methods can be useful for question sequencing. [09%]
(b) State four advantages of observing workers and analysing business documents to determine
system requirements. [04%]
(c) Write three closed-ended questions that you might use in an interview of users of word-
processing package in order to develop ideas for the next version of package. [06%]
[Question 2 continues ...] CSC301G3-2021Continuation of Question 2... 2/3 CSC301G3-2021
(d) List the participants of a Joint Application Design (JAD) session and briefly describe the
responsiblities of each participants. [06%]
[25 % marks]
3. A common form of process model is a Data Flow Diagram (DFD).
(a) Briefly discuss how DFDs can be used as an analysis tool. [06%]
(b) One of the more difficult decisions you need to make when drawing DFDs is when to stop
decomposition processes. State four concrete rules for stopping the decomposition process. [04%]
Consider the following problem description about withdrawal of cash from an Automatic
Teller Machine (ATM):
To start an operation, a card is inserted in the slot, and the personal identification number
(PIN) is entered. The ATM reads information from the card, in particular, the card number
and the expiry date and accepts the PIN from the keyboard input. the card is verified for
expiry date and then if valid, the card number and PIN are sent to the bank for card
validation through communication circuit. The bank either sends confirmation or refusal.
If the confirmation is received, the user is invited to enter a transaction type. Assuming
that the transaction is a withdrawal of cash, one more validation is performed to check that
the amount nominated is below or equal to the daily limit for withdrawals of Rs. 40,000.00,
that the user’s weekly withdrawals do not exceed the weekly limit of Rs 200,000.00, and
that the account balance is in credit to cover the withdrawal. If all these are satisfied the
card is returned, the money is dispensed and withdrawal receipt is printed, with the date,
account number, withdrawal amount and account balance. The bank account records are
updated to reflect the transaction.
i. Draw a context diagram to show how the system interact with the world around it. [05%]
ii. Draw a level-0 data flow diagram. [10%]
[25 % marks]
4. Software validation and software testing are two essential components in the software develop-
ment.
(a) Define what is Software Testing. [03%]
(b) Differentiate the following terms using valid points. [06%]
[Question 4 continues ...] CSC301G3-2021Continuation of Question 4... 3/3 CSC301G3-2021
i. Verification and Validation.
ii. Testing and Debugging.
iii. Black-box testing and White-box testing
(c) State four advantages of automated testing compared to manual testing. [04%]
(d) Explain how beta testing can help in detecting errors that may not have been anticipated
by system builders. [04%]
(e) Software testing process takes place in four stages. List and describe these stages and explain
how each of these testing stages in improving the software quality. [08%]
[25 % marks]
End 31 January 2022
